title: Career Career Organizations
content:
Career Organizations and Resources for CS Students
Morgan State University

---

COLORSTACK
ColorStack's mission is to increase the number of Black, Latinx, and Native American computer science graduates who go on to start rewarding technical careers. Their programs include mentorship, career support, and community building.

Key Resources:
- Career development programs
- Technical interview preparation
- Networking opportunities with tech professionals
- Community support and mentorship

Website: https://www.colorstack.org/
Join: Students can apply for membership on the ColorStack website

---

CODEPATH
CodePath.org is a nonprofit that transforms college CS education to help students find jobs in tech. They offer free courses in mobile development, cybersecurity, and technical interview prep.

Programs Offered:
- iOS Development Course
- Android Development Course
- Cybersecurity Course
- Technical Interview Prep (TIP)

Benefits:
- Free courses for college students
- Industry-relevant curriculum
- Career support and job placement assistance
- Mentorship from tech professionals

Website: https://www.codepath.org/

---

NATIONAL SOCIETY OF BLACK ENGINEERS (NSBE)
NSBE is one of the largest student-governed organizations focused on the academic and professional success of Black engineers and scientists.

Benefits of Membership:
- Networking opportunities at regional and national conferences
- Scholarship opportunities
- Career fairs with major tech companies
- Leadership development programs
- Community service opportunities

Morgan State NSBE Chapter: Contact the School of Engineering for chapter information

Website: https://www.nsbe.org/

---

REWRITING THE CODE
Rewriting the Code is a community of college and early-career women in tech. They provide resources, mentorship, and networking to help women succeed in technical careers.

Programs:
- Fellowship programs with top tech companies
- Community events and workshops
- Mentorship opportunities
- Job and internship postings

Website: https://rewritingthecode.org/

---

MAJOR LEAGUE HACKING (MLH)
MLH is the official student hackathon league. They power over 200 weekend-long hackathons every year, helping students build real software projects.

What MLH Offers:
- Hackathon organization support
- Fellowship programs for summer projects
- Workshops and learning resources
- Hardware and software tools for hackers

Website: https://mlh.io/

---

GRACE HOPPER CELEBRATION (GHC)
The Grace Hopper Celebration is the world's largest gathering of women and non-binary technologists. It features career development, networking, and inspiration from leaders in tech.

What to Expect:
- Keynote speeches from industry leaders
- Technical sessions and workshops
- Career fair with hundreds of companies
- Networking opportunities

Scholarships: GHC Scholarships are available for students to attend the conference
Website: https://ghc.anitab.org/
